##NAMES=Fluorcalciomicrolite
##RRUFFID=R070100
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=(Ca,Na,[box])_2_Ta_2_O_6_F
##LOCALITY=Urucum mine, near Galilea, Minas Gerais, Brazil
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Michael Scott
##DESCRIPTION=Yellow octahedra on ball of colorless stokesite, associated with titanite
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R070100
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Ca_1.30_Na_0.40_U_0.04_Mn_0.02_)(Ta_1.64_Nb_0.15_Ti_0.12_Sn_0.09_)O_6_(F_0.50_O_0.25_)
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 10.463 b: 10.463 c: 10.463 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 90 volume: 1145.3 crystal system: cubic
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
